steps.title: Make a two-row table
steps.content: The problem gives one point and one rate. Since $y=23$ when $x=3$, use the point $(3,23)$. Because every increase of $1$ in $x$ increases $y$ by $7$, the slope is $7$.

steps.title: Find the line from the data
steps.content: Use $y=mx+b$ with $m=7$ and the point $(3,23)$: $$23=7(3)+b$$ $$23=21+b$$ $$b=2$$ So the equation is $y=7x+2$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The matching choice is the only equation with slope $7$ and intercept $2$. A quick check gives $7(3)+2=23$, so C is correct.
